steps.title: Set up the units
steps.content: Keenan made 32 cups of broth total and split it into $x$ small jars and $y$ large jars. The equation $3x + 5y = 32$ totals cups (the right-hand side is in cups), so each term on the left must also be in cups. That forces 3 and 5 to be cup-capacities: 3 cups per small jar and 5 cups per large jar.

steps.title: Interpret $5y$
steps.content: Since $y$ counts large jars and 5 is the cups per large jar, the product $5y$ equals (cups per large jar) $\times$ (number of large jars) $=$ total cups of broth in all the large jars.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) Number of large jars is just $y$, not $5y$. Eliminate.
  • B) Number of small jars is $x$, not $5y$. Eliminate.
  • C) Total cups in large jars $= 5y$. Keep.
  • D) Total cups in small jars is $3x$, not $5y$. Eliminate.
That is the correct answer.
